title: Churruma

'''Churruma''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with deposits of valuable metals that was located within the [[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Mining/Legends|mined]] Churruma's metals and captured the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Chrech Estha]], a respected leader of the planet's [[Unidentified Churruma natives|natives]] who possessed a [[Unidentified stick|carved stick]] that served as a powerful [[Politics/Legends|political]] and religious symbol on his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. The return of Estha and the stick to Churruma sparked a [[Churruma rebellion|rebellion]] that ended the Empire's presence on the planet.

==Description==
Churruma was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="GatORW" /> that was located within the [[Churruma system/Legends|Churruma system]], a part of the [[Velcar sector/Legends|Velcar sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet had underground deposits of precious metals.<ref name="GatORW" />

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] used Churruma to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] its metals, with Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] stationed on the planet. Churruma served the Empire as a major source of raw materials. When the [[Shaman/Legends|shaman]] [[Chrech Estha]], a respected leader of the planet's [[Unidentified Churruma natives|natives]], urged his people to fight against the Empire's presence on the planet, he was captured and [[Transport/Legends|transported]] to the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] planet [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. Although the Empire confiscated the imprisoned leader's [[Unidentified stick|carved stick]], the Imperials did not suspect the artifact's significance to Churruma's natives as a powerful [[Politics/Legends|political]] and religious symbol.<ref name="GatORW" />

{{RPGseed}}
At some point during the Galactic Civil War, a group of individuals brought Estha back to Churruma along with the shaman's stick. His return convinced the planet's remaining leaders to listen to the group's advice and organize a successful planetwide [[Churruma rebellion|rebellion]], ending the Imperial presence on Churruma.<ref name="GatORW" />
{{Endgame}}

Churruma fell within the borders of the [[Pentastar Alignment]] between the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], and was a part of the [[Imperial Remnant]] between [[18 ABY/Legends|18 ABY]] and [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
The relatively unknown Churruma was home to a primitive culture that had several leaders, including the shaman Chrech Estha, who possessed a stick with religious markings carved on it. The stick was a political and religious symbol with the power to either unite or divide Churruma's society.<ref name="GatORW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Churruma was first mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. The book includes an adventure seed for use with the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', and this article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="GatORW" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed Churruma in grid square J-5.<ref name="TEA" />
